Operation 'games.recall.unlinkPersona' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_output_details. Original description lacks a summary entirely and is unclear about the operation's purpose and effects. Added summary to clarify the action. Enhanced description to explain what a Recall token represents, clarify the identification methods, explain the side effect (account unlinking), and specify the response behavior. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description: Delete a Recall token linking the PGS Player principal identified by the Recall session and an in-game account identified either by the 'persona' or by the token value.
data.summary_suggestion: Unlink in-game account from Recall session
data.description_suggestion: Deletes a Recall token that links a PGS Player principal (identified by the current Recall session) to an in-game account. The in-game account can be identified either by the 'persona' field or by the token value in the request. After unlinking, the player's in-game account will no longer be associated with their PGS identity. Returns a 200 response on successful deletion.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks a summary entirely and is unclear about the operation's purpose and effects. Added summary to clarify the action. Enhanced description to explain what a Recall token represents, clarify the identification methods, explain the side effect (account unlinking), and specify the response behavior. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description: Checks whether the games client is out of date.
data.summary_suggestion: Check if games client version is current
data.description_suggestion: Checks whether the authenticated user's games client is out of date by comparing the client version against the current required version. Requires the client version as a parameter. Returns revision information indicating whether an update is needed and details about the current and required versions. Used to determine if the client should prompt the user to update.
